repo_notes.content: Synced from DeepWiki: https://deepwiki.com/miyamaerei/AutoCodeForge/1-autocodeforge-overview on 2026-05-19. Source index snapshot referenced commit 770d83.

pages.title: AutoCodeForge Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to AutoCodeForge: what it is, its purpose as an AI-driven software development console, the MVP scope, and how the major modules fit together. Links to all child sections for deeper coverage.

page_notes.content: AutoCodeForge is an AI-driven software development console that converts natural-language requirements into executable coding tasks, with real-time execution visibility and artifact management. MVP scope emphasizes a runnable interactive frontend decoupled from backend via mock data.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Setup instructions, development environment, npm scripts (dev, build, test:unit), environment variables (VITE_API_BASE_URL), and the USE_MOCK toggle. Covers the Vite + Vue 3 + TypeScript toolchain.

page_notes.content: Development uses Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite with npm scripts dev/build/test:unit and runtime toggles like USE_MOCK and VITE_API_BASE_URL for environment behavior.

pages.title: Application Shell & Navigation
pages.purpose: Describes the root App.vue layout, the AppSidebar component, the two navigation modes (User vs Settings), MenuItem data structure, context-switching logic, and the global router setup in src/router/index.ts.

page_notes.content: App.vue composes global layout and AppSidebar, switching between user and settings contexts by route prefix. Router-view hosts module screens while menu data drives navigation state.

pages.title: Module Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the module-first architecture pattern used across all feature modules. Introduces the standard directory layout, naming conventions, data-flow layers (API -> Mapper -> Store -> View), and the GitHub Copilot skill system. Links to child pages for each architectural layer.

page_notes.content: The codebase follows a module-first pattern where each feature owns API calls, store, routes, and views, enabling clear boundaries and scalable extension.

pages.title: Module Directory Layout & Naming Conventions
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for the enforced module-first folder structure: api/, models/, store/, views/, routes.ts, index.ts. Covers DTO vs Model naming, mapper functions (dtoToModel/modelToDto), and the barrel-export pattern via index.ts.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Detailed reference for the enforced module-first folder structure: api/, models/, store/, views/, routes.ts, index.ts. Covers DTO vs Model naming, mapper functions (dtoToModel/modelToDto), and the barrel-export pattern via index.ts.

pages.title: State Management Pattern (Pinia Setup Stores)
pages.purpose: Explains the Pinia setup-store pattern used in every module: loading/error/data state lifecycle, try/catch/finally actions, storeToRefs usage in views, and the hasData guard to prevent redundant fetches. References useTaskCenterStore, usePipelineCenterStore, and useRepoManagementStore as concrete examples.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Explains the Pinia setup-store pattern used in every module: loading/error/data state lifecycle, try/catch/finally actions, storeToRefs usage in views, and the hasData guard to prevent redundant fetches. References useTaskCenterStore, usePipelineCenterStore, and useRepoManagementStore as concrete examples.

pages.title: API Layer & Mock System
pages.purpose: Covers the shared request.ts Axios wrapper, the USE_MOCK toggle in src/config/runtime.ts, the mock data layer (src/mock/), and how each module's .api.ts file branches between live HTTP calls and mock functions. Explains simulated latency via sleep().

page_notes.content: Runtime data access routes through a shared request layer and can branch to src/mock providers when USE_MOCK is enabled, supporting backend-independent demos and development.

pages.title: Routing Conventions
pages.purpose: Documents the Vue Router setup, route naming pattern (<module>.<action>), lazy-loading with dynamic imports, meta.requiresAuth on every route, and how module route arrays are composed into the global router. Covers all six module route files.

page_notes.content: Routes are declared per module and merged centrally, with lazy-loaded views and consistent naming/meta conventions (including auth-related metadata).

pages.title: Console Module (AI Dev Console)
pages.purpose: Overview of the console module - the primary entry point for the AI development assistant. Introduces the ConsoleEntryView dashboard and links to child pages covering the chat interface, code review, wiki, and automations sub-views.

page_notes.content: Console is the primary AI interaction surface, including chat-first workflows and shortcuts to review/wiki/automation functions.

pages.title: Unified Chat Interface (Session & Ask Modes)
pages.purpose: Deep dive into ConsoleChatView.vue, the useConsoleChat composable, and the UnifiedChatPanel component. Covers session mode vs ask mode, session lifecycle (create/select/delete), suggested questions, chat option chips, and the getChatOptionsByModule configuration.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Deep dive into ConsoleChatView.vue, the useConsoleChat composable, and the UnifiedChatPanel component. Covers session mode vs ask mode, session lifecycle (create/select/delete), suggested questions, chat option chips, and the getChatOptionsByModule configuration.

pages.title: Console Sub-Views: Review, Wiki & Automations
pages.purpose: Covers the three specialized console views: ConsoleReviewView (PR management and code review), ConsoleWikiView (repository documentation browser), and ConsoleAutomationsView (AI automation workflow management). Explains their data structures and UI patterns.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers the three specialized console views: ConsoleReviewView (PR management and code review), ConsoleWikiView (repository documentation browser), and ConsoleAutomationsView (AI automation workflow management). Explains their data structures and UI patterns.

pages.title: AI Task Center Module
pages.purpose: Overview of the AI Task Center - the core module for creating and monitoring AI-driven coding tasks. Introduces the three-panel layout, task lifecycle states, and links to child pages for the store/API layer and individual views.

page_notes.content: Task Center orchestrates task creation, execution tracking, logs, chat, and diff feedback to make AI coding runs observable end-to-end.

pages.title: Task Center Views & Lifecycle
pages.purpose: Covers TaskCenterListView (tabular task list), TaskCenterCreateView (form-based task creation with validation and redirect), TaskCenterView (three-panel dashboard: task menu, AI chat, execution feedback), and TaskCenterDetailView. Explains task states: running, completed, paused, failed.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers TaskCenterListView (tabular task list), TaskCenterCreateView (form-based task creation with validation and redirect), TaskCenterView (three-panel dashboard: task menu, AI chat, execution feedback), and TaskCenterDetailView. Explains task states: running, completed, paused, failed.

pages.title: Task Center Store & API
pages.purpose: Documents useTaskCenterStore state properties (tasks, selectedTask, logs, chat, diff), actions (loadTasks, loadTaskDetail with Promise.all, submitTask, sendTaskChat), and the task-center.api.ts endpoints. Covers DTOs: TaskSummaryDto, TaskDetailDto, TaskCreateRequestDto, TaskLogDto, ChatMessageDto, TaskDiffDto.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Documents useTaskCenterStore state properties (tasks, selectedTask, logs, chat, diff), actions (loadTasks, loadTaskDetail with Promise.all, submitTask, sendTaskChat), and the task-center.api.ts endpoints. Covers DTOs: TaskSummaryDto, TaskDetailDto, TaskCreateRequestDto, TaskLogDto, ChatMessageDto, TaskDiffDto.

pages.title: Repository Management Module
pages.purpose: Overview of the repo-management module: its purpose for managing VCS repositories, branches, and pull requests. Links to child pages for views and the store/API layer.

page_notes.content: Repo Management focuses on repository inventory, branch data, and pull-request visibility to support VCS operations alongside AI workflows.

pages.title: Repository Management Views
pages.purpose: Covers RepoManagementView (overview dashboard with 17:7 grid layout), RepoManagementListView (repository table), RepoManagementBranchesView (branch details with commit/author data), and RepoManagementPRsView (pull request list). Explains the fetch-on-mount pattern and empty/loading/error state machine.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers RepoManagementView (overview dashboard with 17:7 grid layout), RepoManagementListView (repository table), RepoManagementBranchesView (branch details with commit/author data), and RepoManagementPRsView (pull request list). Explains the fetch-on-mount pattern and empty/loading/error state machine.

pages.title: Repository Management Store & API
pages.purpose: Documents useRepoManagementStore (repositories, branches, loading, error, hasRepositories, hasBranches), repo-management.api.ts endpoints, and the mock data from src/mock/project.ts (RepositoryDto, BranchDto, PullRequestDto).

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Documents useRepoManagementStore (repositories, branches, loading, error, hasRepositories, hasBranches), repo-management.api.ts endpoints, and the mock data from src/mock/project.ts (RepositoryDto, BranchDto, PullRequestDto).

pages.title: Pipeline Center Module
pages.purpose: Overview of the pipeline-center module for CI/CD monitoring. Introduces pipelines vs builds, status indicators, and links to child pages for views and the store/API.

page_notes.content: Pipeline Center monitors CI/CD pipelines and build runs with status-centric views for operational transparency.

pages.title: Pipeline Center Views
pages.purpose: Covers PipelineCenterListView (pipeline table with success rate and rerun actions), PipelineCenterBuildsView (build history with duration and branch data), and PipelineCenterView (real-time execution dashboard with el-steps for Pull/Install/Test/Build stages and log output).

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers PipelineCenterListView (pipeline table with success rate and rerun actions), PipelineCenterBuildsView (build history with duration and branch data), and PipelineCenterView (real-time execution dashboard with el-steps for Pull/Install/Test/Build stages and log output).

pages.title: Pipeline Center Store & API
pages.purpose: Documents usePipelineCenterStore state (pipelines, builds, loading, error, hasPipelines, hasBuilds), pipeline-center.api.ts endpoints (fetchPipelines, fetchBuilds), PipelineDto and BuildDto structures, and the statusColorMap for UI rendering.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Documents usePipelineCenterStore state (pipelines, builds, loading, error, hasPipelines, hasBuilds), pipeline-center.api.ts endpoints (fetchPipelines, fetchBuilds), PipelineDto and BuildDto structures, and the statusColorMap for UI rendering.

pages.title: Dashboard Module
pages.purpose: Covers the DashboardView.vue: ECharts-based task trend visualization (tasks created vs successes), DashboardStatsDto (tasksToday, successRate, runningCount, alertCount), and the overall health alert panel. Explains the getDashboardStats and getRecentTasks mock functions.

page_notes.content: Dashboard aggregates high-level health metrics and trend charts for quick system status awareness.

pages.title: System Configuration Module
pages.purpose: Overview of the system-config module and its eleven settings views under /settings/*. Introduces the two categories: operational settings (preferences, schedules, users) and integration settings (repositories, API keys, models, DeepWiki). Links to child pages for each group.

page_notes.content: System Config centralizes operational preferences plus integrations such as API keys, model settings, and related governance controls.

pages.title: Operational Settings Views
pages.purpose: Covers SystemConfigPreferencesView (locale, timezone, theme, landingPage, notifications), SystemConfigSchedulesView (cron expressions, retryLimit, alertChannel, timezone validation), SystemConfigUsersView (user management), and SystemConfigReviewView (minApprovals, requiredChecks, SLA, blockOnFailingChecks, enforceCodeOwners).

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers SystemConfigPreferencesView (locale, timezone, theme, landingPage, notifications), SystemConfigSchedulesView (cron expressions, retryLimit, alertChannel, timezone validation), SystemConfigUsersView (user management), and SystemConfigReviewView (minApprovals, requiredChecks, SLA, blockOnFailingChecks, enforceCodeOwners).

pages.title: Integration & AI Settings Views
pages.purpose: Covers SystemConfigRepositoriesView (provider, owner, authMode, mergeStrategies, requireChecks), SystemConfigApiView (API key management for OpenAI/GitHub/Azure), SystemConfigModelsView (LLM model selection: GPT-4, Claude, Llama), SystemConfigSkillView (execution mode, timeoutSeconds, allowWriteFiles, allowTerminal), SystemConfigDeepWikiView (embedding model, topK, metric, autoReindex), and SystemConfigKnowledgeView.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers SystemConfigRepositoriesView (provider, owner, authMode, mergeStrategies, requireChecks), SystemConfigApiView (API key management for OpenAI/GitHub/Azure), SystemConfigModelsView (LLM model selection: GPT-4, Claude, Llama), SystemConfigSkillView (execution mode, timeoutSeconds, allowWriteFiles, allowTerminal), SystemConfigDeepWikiView (embedding model, topK, metric, autoReindex), and SystemConfigKnowledgeView.

pages.title: UI Component Library & Styling
pages.purpose: Overview of shared UI components and the global styling system. Links to child pages for the component catalog and the CSS design token system.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Overview of shared UI components and the global styling system. Links to child pages for the component catalog and the CSS design token system.

pages.title: Shared Components
pages.purpose: Documents the reusable components in src/components/: UnifiedChatPanel (message board, composer, option chips, auto-scroll), AppSidebar (MenuItem interface, active state logic, avatar pulse animation), WelcomeItem (icon/heading/default slots, timeline CSS), TheWelcome, HelloWorld, and the icon components in src/components/icons/.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Documents the reusable components in src/components/: UnifiedChatPanel (message board, composer, option chips, auto-scroll), AppSidebar (MenuItem interface, active state logic, avatar pulse animation), WelcomeItem (icon/heading/default slots, timeline CSS), TheWelcome, HelloWorld, and the icon components in src/components/icons/.

pages.title: CSS Design Tokens & Theming
pages.purpose: Explains the two-tier CSS variable system: palette variables (--vt-c-*) and semantic variables (--color-*). Covers light/dark mode via prefers-color-scheme, the app surface/background tokens in theme.css (--app-bg, --app-surface), Element Plus overrides, and the Inter font stack.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Explains the two-tier CSS variable system: palette variables (--vt-c-*) and semantic variables (--color-*). Covers light/dark mode via prefers-color-scheme, the app surface/background tokens in theme.css (--app-bg, --app-surface), Element Plus overrides, and the Inter font stack.

pages.title: Developer Tooling & Build Infrastructure
pages.purpose: Overview of the build and test infrastructure. Links to child pages for TypeScript configuration and the Vite/Vitest setup.

page_notes.content: Build/test foundation is Vite + TypeScript + Vitest with project-reference tsconfig structure and workspace scripts for development and validation.

pages.title: TypeScript Configuration
pages.purpose: Documents the Project References architecture: tsconfig.json (orchestrator), tsconfig.app.json (production source, @/* alias, noUncheckedIndexedAccess), tsconfig.vitest.json (jsdom+node types, includes __tests__), and tsconfig.node.json (Node 24 tooling isolation). Explains tsBuildInfoFile incremental caching.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Documents the Project References architecture: tsconfig.json (orchestrator), tsconfig.app.json (production source, @/* alias, noUncheckedIndexedAccess), tsconfig.vitest.json (jsdom+node types, includes __tests__), and tsconfig.node.json (Node 24 tooling isolation). Explains tsBuildInfoFile incremental caching.

pages.title: Vite Build & Vitest Testing
pages.purpose: Covers vite.config.ts (plugins: @vitejs/plugin-vue, vite-plugin-vue-devtools; @/* alias resolution), vitest.config.ts setup, npm scripts (dev, build, build-only, type-check, test:unit), and the HelloWorld.spec.ts test as an example of the testing pattern.

page_notes.content: DeepWiki sync note: Covers vite.config.ts (plugins: @vitejs/plugin-vue, vite-plugin-vue-devtools; @/* alias resolution), vitest.config.ts setup, npm scripts (dev, build, build-only, type-check, test:unit), and the HelloWorld.spec.ts test as an example of the testing pattern.
